user_story: As the system, I want to inject the subscriber's Core mindfulness state into their USER.md so that the AI assistant is aware of recent meditation sessions when composing responses.
expected_behaviour: Registered at order=41 under key 'core' via @register_section. Only rendered when tenant.core_enabled=True. Queries READY MeditationSession rows for the tenant. Emits up to 2 lines: last meditation (title, date, theme[:120]) and a 7-day session count. Returns empty string if no sessions. Output capped at 600 chars. Triggered automatically on MeditationSession model changes (create/save) via the refresh_on=(MeditationSession,) registration. Output section heading is '## Core — mindfulness state'.
inventory_notes: The 7-day count uses the tenant's local date (tenant_today) to match the locally-stamped session dates, avoiding UTC off-by-one on evening sessions.

feature: iOS Core AI On-Device Model Manifest
user_story: As a subscriber on iOS, I want my app to fetch the on-device AI model manifest so that it can download and verify the correct model bundle for local inference.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/coreai/model/manifest/ requires JWT auth (IsAuthenticated). Reads COREAI_MODEL_BASE_URL and COREAI_MODEL_MANIFEST_PATH from settings. If either is empty or the manifest file does not exist, returns 404 {detail: 'No on-device model is configured.'} so the iOS app falls back gracefully. Parses the JSON manifest file; for each file entry builds an absolute URL as {base_url}/{name}/{version}/{url-encoded-path} and adds sha256 + bytes. Returns {name, version, total_bytes, files: [{path, url, sha256, bytes}]}. If the manifest file is unreadable or malformed (missing keys), returns 503.
dependencies: Local filesystem (COREAI_MODEL_MANIFEST_PATH), Azure Blob/CDN (COREAI_MODEL_BASE_URL, for downloads — not called by this endpoint)
inventory_notes: 503 on corrupt manifest is intentional fail-closed behavior: the iOS client verifies each file's sha256, so a partial manifest would yield an unverifiable download. The manifest JSON is generated by the generate_coreai_manifest management command, not at runtime.

feature: Management Command: Generate Core AI Model Manifest
user_story: As an operator, I want to generate the on-device AI model manifest JSON from an exported LanguageBundle directory so that the iOS app can verify and download the model files.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py generate_coreai_manifest --bundle <dir> --name <name> --model-version <version> --out <path>. Walks the bundle directory recursively, computing streaming SHA-256 and byte size for every file (skipping .DS_Store). Writes a JSON manifest with {name, version, total_bytes, files: [{path, sha256, bytes}]} to the --out path. Raises CommandError if the bundle directory does not exist or contains no files. Prints a success message with file count, total GB, and the expected upload path pattern.
inventory_notes: Operator must separately upload the bundle files to COREAI_MODEL_BASE_URL/<name>/<version>/<path>. The --version flag is named --model-version because Django's BaseCommand reserves --version.

user_story: As an admin, I want to view and edit CoreProfile records so that I can diagnose onboarding issues and manually correct mindfulness preferences.
expected_behaviour: Django admin at /admin/core/coreprofile/. list_display: tenant, onboarding_status, preferred_voice, daily_cron_enabled, created_at. list_filter: onboarding_status, ambient_bed_enabled, daily_cron_enabled. search_fields: tenant__user__display_name. readonly_fields: id, created_at, updated_at. Standard admin create/update/delete.

user_story: As an admin, I want to view and inspect MeditationSession records so that I can diagnose render failures and audit generated content.
expected_behaviour: Django admin at /admin/core/meditationsession/. list_display: title, tenant, date, status, voice, duration_ms, created_at. list_filter: status. search_fields: title, theme, tenant__user__display_name. readonly_fields: id, created_at, updated_at. The error field is visible for failed sessions to expose the last render error message.
